rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical deuteration/functionalization of alkenes using CDCl3 as a deuterated dichloromethyl radical source
electrochemical_role: Anodic/cathodic generation of dichloromethyl-d radical (·CDCl2) from CDCl3, which adds to the alkene as the first step
electron_flow: Electrolysis homolyzes CDCl3 to generate a ·CDCl2 radical which adds to the styrene C=C; the resulting benzylic radical either cyclizes/closes to give cyclopropane 2ag or dimerizes to S1, and in fluorinated substrate S2 defluorination delivers S3, confirming radical addition as the initiating step.

probe_reagents.name: styrene (radical acceptor probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction with CDCl3 under standard conditions gave cyclopropane 2ag and isolable dimer S1, indicating ·CDCl2 addition to alkene

probe_reagents.name: fluorinated substrate S2
probe_reagents.observation: Defluorination product S3 was obtained, consistent with radical addition of ·CDCl2 followed by β-F loss

notes: Mechanism supported by isolation of dimer S1 (radical–radical coupling byproduct) and defluorinated product S3 from a fluorinated probe; both implicate ·CDCl2 radical as the chain-initiating species. Comparison table also shows electrochemistry gives higher D-incorporation (96–98%) at low CDCl3 loading vs. non-electrochemical conditions.
